content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Verified the amendment's claims against the actual code. The bug is real: rebase_task_worktree is called unconditionally at lines 681-687 (multi-repo) and 1408-1414 (single-repo) before execution reaches the RunStatus::Completed bypass at line 3007. When a reconciliation failure causes retry_task to move the task back to Pending, the subsequent claim re-enters the full processing path including rebase. If the default branch has advanced, rebase_task_worktree fails at line 2690-2703 (fail_claimed_task_preserve_worktree + return Err), the caller catches the error and returns early (line 706 / line 1416), and the RunStatus::Completed branch is never reached. This makes transient reconciliation failures (br close, sync, milestone update) permanently unrecoverable — a real bug deserving a fix.

## Votes

- `fr-13-fe6f0a4c`: Accept
  Confirmed by code inspection. The control flow is: (1) retry_task at task_service.rs:476 moves Failed→Pending, (2) claim_task resumes the preserved branch, (3) rebase_task_worktree is called unconditionally at daemon_loop.rs:681/1408, (4) if the default branch advanced, rebase fails and fail_claimed_task_preserve_worktree at line 2690 marks the task Failed with rebase_conflict, returning Err, (5) the callers at lines 706/1416 catch the error and return early, (6) the RunStatus::Completed bypass at line 3007 is never reached, (7) try_reconcile_success is never retried. The rebase is unnecessary for reconciliation-only retries since bead close, sync, and milestone updates are metadata operations independent of worktree content. A dedicated pre-rebase check that detects a Completed run snapshot and skips directly to reconciliation is the correct fix. The regression test is also warranted to guard this path.
